alignments.rationale: The preprint credits Relexi with enabling efficient solver–RL interaction, while the publication reframes this as a general requirement for such interactions, reducing the specific positive attribution to Relexi.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of various HPC solvers is facilitated by the in-memory data transfer provided by the SmartSim library. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Relexi allows easy integration of various HPC solvers through in-memory data transfer provided by the SmartSim library. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same point but frames it as a feature of Relexi ("allows easy integration") and explicitly names SmartSim, clarifying the integration mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An RL-augmented CFD solver can find a control strategy for optimal eddy viscosity selection in large eddy simulations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An RL-augmented CFD solver can find a control strategy for optimal eddy viscosity selection in large eddy simulations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same result about using RL to find control strategies for eddy viscosity selection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hybrid data driven-physics constrained Gaussian process regression framework reduces the amount of data required while improving accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hybrid data-driven-physics-constrained Gaussian process regression framework reduces the amount of data required for accurate predictions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (reduced data requirements with accurate predictions) with near-identical wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model incorporates physics knowledge using Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution and is derived through a maximum likelihood approach.
alignments.rationale: The specific methodological detail (Boltzmann–Gibbs formulation and MLE derivation) does not appear among the publication claims and is therefore treated as removed from the high-level claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model achieves good results in high-dimensional problems and correctly propagates uncertainty with very limited labeled data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model achieves good results in high-dimensional problems while correctly propagating uncertainty.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the high-dimensional and uncertainty-propagation parts but omits the explicit ‘‘very limited labeled data’’ qualifier in this claim (that aspect is stated separately in Q3).

alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating physics constraints into Gaussian process regression improves the accuracy of the model.
alignments.rationale: This explicit, general claim about the effect of physics constraints on accuracy is presented in the publication but was not stated as a standalone claim in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model requires very limited labeled data to function effectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication isolates the ‘‘very limited labeled data’’ point as its own claim (Q3); while the preprint mentioned limited data as part of P2, the published claims present it separately, so it is treated here as an added standalone publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed NN-EUCLID approach enables unsupervised learning of hyperelastic constitutive laws using only full-field displacement and global reaction force data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed NN-EUCLID approach enables unsupervised learning of hyperelastic constitutive laws using only full-field displacement and global reaction force data.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim verbatim in scope and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The absence of stress labels in the learning process is compensated by a physics-motivated loss function based on the conservation of linear momentum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The absence of stress labels in the learning process is compensated by a physics-motivated loss function based on the conservation of linear momentum.
alignments.rationale: Identical formulation and emphasis are preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network architecture used in NN-EUCLID automatically satisfies multiple physical and thermodynamic constraints for hyperelastic constitutive laws.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network architecture automatically satisfies multiple physical and thermodynamic constraints for hyperelastic constitutive laws.
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved without substantive change between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach can accurately learn several hidden isotropic and anisotropic hyperelastic constitutive laws without using stress data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach can accurately learn several hidden isotropic and anisotropic hyperelastic constitutive laws without using stress data.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about learning multiple constitutive laws without stress labels.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network-based constitutive models demonstrate good generalization capability beyond the strain states observed during training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network-based constitutive models demonstrate good generalization capability beyond the strain states observed during training.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the generalization claim with the same moderate certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gaussian Process Regression Networks (GPRNs) are capable of jointly modelling radial velocity data and traditional stellar activity indicators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gaussian Process Regression Networks (GPRNs) are capable of jointly modelling radial velocity data and traditional stellar activity indicators.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same capability claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simplest GPRN configuration can describe the behaviour of solar radial velocity data at least as accurately as previously published methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simplest GPRN configuration can describe the behaviour of solar radial velocity data at least as accurately as previously published methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The correlation between the radial velocity and stellar activity time series reaches a maximum at separations of a few days.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The correlation between the radial velocity and stellar activity time series reaches a maximum at separations of a few days.
alignments.rationale: The observed correlation-timescale claim is carried over with the same uncertainty level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is evidence of non-stationary behaviour in the time series, associated with an approaching solar activity minimum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is evidence of non-stationary behaviour in the time series, associated with an approaching solar activity minimum.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's observation of non-stationarity and its likely association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Decoding Network (DECONET) outperforms the baselines consistently across all datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Decoding Network (DECONET) outperforms the baselines consistently across all datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical results associated with DECONET are validated through experimental assessments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical results associated with DECONET are validated through experimental assessments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DECONET jointly learns a decoder and a redundant sparsifying analysis operator shared across its layers.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Rademacher complexity of DECONET is estimated to provide upper bounds for its generalization error.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised assisted active learning framework improves sample selection for biomedical image segmentation without requiring labels for model initialization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The segmentation model is first warmed up with self-supervised learning before sample selection is performed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed methodology achieves promising performance with substantial improvements over existing baselines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed methodology achieves promising performance with substantial improvements over existing baselines.
alignments.rationale: The experimental-result claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can save annotation costs in clinical practice by smartly selecting samples to annotate.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proof-of-concept method, GAN-Aimbot, improves player performance while remaining hidden from both automatic and manual protection mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed unifying deep unfolding multi-sampling-ratio interpretable CS-MRI framework offers more generalizability than previous works. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed and evaluated transformer-based classifiers to identify fake news using eight state-of-the-art Arabic contextualized embedding models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transformer-based classifiers have been developed and evaluated for identifying fake news using eight state-of-the-art Arabic contextualized embedding models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing transformer-based classifiers and the eight models is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results confirm that these state-of-the-art models are robust, with accuracy exceeding 98%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results confirm that the state-of-the-art models for Arabic fake news detection are robust, achieving accuracy exceeding 98%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The high-accuracy experimental result is preserved in the published paper with equivalent wording.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Research on fake news detection in the Arabic language is scarce compared to studies conducted in English. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This contextual claim about the scarcity of Arabic-language research is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint; it adds background motivation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradient-based techniques can effectively solve bilevel optimization problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient descent can be effectively used to solve learning problems in bilevel optimization by leveraging implicit differentiation and the equilibrium propagation theorem.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the effectiveness of gradient-based methods but specifies they are applied to learning problems and explicitly names the two mechanisms used, which clarifies and narrows the original general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bilevel optimization provides greater modeling flexibility compared to explicit definitions of systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The characterization of bilevel optimization allows for greater modeling flexibility compared to the usual explicit definition of such systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about increased modeling flexibility with essentially the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper distinguishes gradient-based approaches into two categories: those rooted in implicit differentiation and those leveraging the equilibrium propagation theorem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient descent can be effectively used to solve learning problems in bilevel optimization by leveraging implicit differentiation and the equilibrium propagation theorem.
alignments.rationale: Both versions identify the two methodological roots (implicit differentiation and equilibrium propagation); the publication frames this in the context of how gradient descent can leverage them.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents mathematical foundations and gradient-estimation algorithms for solving bilevel optimization problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review presents the mathematical foundations behind gradient estimation algorithms and compares the competitive advantages of different approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim about presenting mathematical foundations and algorithms but explicitly adds comparative analysis, clarifying the contribution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Bilevel optimization extends the loss minimization framework underlying statistical learning to systems that are implicitly defined through a quantity they minimize.
alignments.rationale: This conceptual framing of bilevel optimization as an extension of loss-minimization was not stated in the preprint and appears as an added contextual conclusion in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The investigation into interactive model cards reveals strengths and limitations compared to standard model cards.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The investigation identified several conceptual dimensions that summarize the strengths and limitations of standard and interactive model cards.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's result, summarizing identified strengths and limitations along conceptual dimensions without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Carefully considered design and interactivity are crucial for supporting non-expert analysts using deep learning models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings demonstrate the importance of carefully considered design and interactivity for orienting and supporting non-expert analysts using deep learning models.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same conclusion about the importance of design and interactivity for non-expert users with no notable change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Design elements such as language, visual cues, and warnings enhance interactivity and accessibility of non-interactive content.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have identified design elements, such as language, visual cues, and warnings, that support interactivity and make non-interactive content accessible.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the identified design elements and their roles; the claim is preserved in meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the need to consider broader sociotechnical contexts and organizational dynamics in AI/ML documentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the need for consideration of broader sociotechnical contexts and organizational dynamics in AI/ML documentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about sociotechnical and organizational considerations with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quality of thirty biomedical ontologies and the Computer Science Ontology was investigated from the perspective of a practical use case.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quality of thirty biomedical ontologies and the Computer Science Ontology was investigated from the perspective of a practical use case.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's exact result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diverse methods to detect potential issues in ontologies, including natural language processing and network analysis, are proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diverse methods to detect potential issues in ontologies, including natural language processing and network analysis, are proposed.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Advancing automatic tools for ontology quality assurance are crucial for ontology improvement but will not solve the problem entirely.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Advancing automatic tools for ontology quality assurance are crucial for ontology improvement but will not solve the problem entirely.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about the role and limits of automatic QA tools.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ontology reuse is the ultimate method for continuously verifying and improving ontology quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ontology reuse is the ultimate method for continuously verifying and improving ontology quality.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's conclusion about ontology reuse exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple issues in ontologies can be found and fixed primarily through practical and diverse ontology reuse scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multiple issues in ontologies can be found and fixed primarily through practical and diverse ontology reuse scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same result but with higher stated certainty, so confidence is marked as amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The challenge of training persists even when the boundary conditions are strictly enforced.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The complexity of training persists even when the boundary conditions are strictly enforced.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same result about persistent training complexity under strict boundary conditions with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training challenge is closely related to the Kolmogorov n-width associated with problems demonstrating transport, convection, traveling waves, or moving fronts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The complexity of training is closely related to the Kolmogorov n-width associated with problems demonstrating transport, convection, traveling waves, or moving fronts.
alignments.rationale: Both versions link the training difficulty to Kolmogorov n-width for the same class of problems; the publication restates this result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed LPINNs architecture conforms to the causality innate to convection and leverages the direction of travel of information in the domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LPINNs provide a PDE-informed solution that reduces the complexity of convection-dominated PINNs at the PDE level.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the outcome (reduced complexity at the PDE level) rather than the mechanistic description (conforming to causality and leveraging information travel), shifting the claim's focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The loss landscapes of LPINNs are less sensitive to the complexity of the problems compared to those in traditional PINNs in the Eulerian framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The loss landscapes of LPINNs are less sensitive to the complexity of convection problems compared to those in traditional PINNs in the Eulerian framework.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the result about loss landscape sensitivity with equivalent meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthetic colonoscopy images generated by Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) inversion can be used as training data to improve the lesion classification performance of deep learning models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetic colonoscopy images generated by Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) inversion can be used as training data to improve polyp classification performance by deep learning models.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core result but specifies 'polyp' classification rather than the broader 'lesion' term, narrowing and clarifying the target task.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach outperforms comparative colonoscopy data augmentation techniques without the need to re-train multiple generative models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach outperforms other colonoscopy data augmentation techniques and does not require re-training multiple generative models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the performance and practical advantage (no re-training) of the approach with the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach can perform multiple colonoscopy data augmentations, which improve the downstream polyp classification performance over baseline and comparison methods by up to 6%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GAN inversion can produce multiple colonoscopy data augmentations that improve the downstream polyp classification performance by 2.7% in F1-score and 4.9% in sensitivity over other methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the 'up to 6%' characterization with specific, smaller numerical improvements, thereby narrowing the claimed magnitude and tempering confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Testing on unseen out-of-domain data showcased an improvement of 2.9% in F1-score and 2.7% in sensitivity.
alignments.rationale: The out-of-domain testing results (specific improvements) appear in the publication but were not present in the preprint, so this is an added empirical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-level visual feature learning method effectively extracts language-guided holistic and regional image features for remote sensing visual question answering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-level visual feature learning method effectively extracts language-guided holistic and regional image features for remote sensing visual question answering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim texts are effectively identical in content and certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A self-paced curriculum learning (SPCL)-based VQA model is developed to train networks with samples in an easy-to-hard manner. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed RSVQA framework demonstrates promising performance when evaluated on three public datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method introduces a novel collision-aware concavity metric that is more robust in detecting various approximation errors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Employing a multi-step tree search for determining cutting planes leads to a globally better solution and avoids unnecessary cuttings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The PO4AO method is quick to train, typically taking 5–10 seconds.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed metrics correlate as expected with the existing cohesion indices.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, partial image phase-only correlation, aims to automatically quantify joint space narrowing (JSN) progression in early rheumatoid arthritis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean error in measuring JSN progression can be reduced to 0.0130 mm when applied to phantom radiographs with ground truth. (certainty=high, type=result)

(certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BNN is more stable and robust to model pruning compared to conventional models. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The uncertainty is higher for samples with higher expected prediction error when using BNNs. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed LRTC-TSpN method effectively imputes missing spatiotemporal traffic data under various missing cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed LRTC-TSpN method achieves up to a 52% improvement in mean absolute error (MAE) compared to baseline methods even when the missing ratio is as high as 90%. (certainty=high, type=result)
